title: Composite electrodes and methods for the fabrication and use thereof
abstract: Disclosed herein are precursor compounds, composite electrodes comprising the same, and methods of making and use thereof.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A method for forming a composite electrode, comprising: depositing a precursor solution onto a substrate, thereby forming a precursor electrode comprising a precursor layer disposed on the substrate; and thermally annealing the precursor electrode, thereby forming a composite electrode comprising a M0S2 layer disposed on the substrate; wherein the substrate comprises a semiconductor substrate and/or a conductive substrate; and wherein the precursor solution comprises a precursor compound having a formula I: [Mo3S y (S 2 CNR2)z]X4-z I wherein y is 4 or 7; z is 3 or 4, with the proviso that when y is 7, z is 3; R is a substituted or unsubstituted C 1 -C 15 alkyl group, a substituted or unsubstituted C 2 -C 15 alkenyl group, a substituted or unsubstituted C 2 -C 15 alkynyl group, or a substituted or unsubstituted C 3 -C 20 aryl group; and X is a halide anion, a tetrafluoroborate anion, or a hexafluorophosphate anion. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the precursor compound has a formula A: A 

 wherein the dotted lines between S, C, and N represent single or double bonds, as valence permits. 3. The method of claim 1, wherein the precursor compound has a formula B: B wherein the dotted lines between S, C, and N represent single or double bonds, as valence permits. 4. The method of any one of claims 1-3, wherein X is a halide anion. 5. The method of any one of claims 1-4, wherein X is an iodine anion. 6. The method of claim 1, wherein the precursor compound has a formula C: C wherein the dotted lines between S and C, and C and N represent single or double bonds, as valence permits. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein R is a substituted or unsubstituted C1-C4 alkyl. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein R is an unsubstituted Ci-C 4 alkyl. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-8, wherein R is ethyl. 10. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein R is a substituted or unsubstituted Cio-Cu aryl. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein R is a substituted C10-C15 aryl. 12. The method of claim 10 or claim 11, wherein R is 3,5-di-ieri-butylbenzyl. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the concentration of the precursor compound in the precursor solution is from 10 nM to 50.0 mM, from 10 nM to 1 mM, or from 0.25 mM to 5.0 mM. 14. The method of any one of claims 1-13, wherein the precursor solution further comprises a solvent comprising tetrahydrofuran (THF), dimethylformamide (DMF), dichloromethane (CH2CI2), ethylene glycol, ethanol, methanol, water, acetonitrile, chloroform, toluene, methyl acetate, ethyl acetate, or combinations thereof. 15. The method of any one of claims 1-14, wherein the precursor solution is deposited onto the substrate by spin coating, spray coating, physical vapor deposition, chemical vapor deposition, or a combination thereof. 16. The method of any one of claims 1-15, wherein the precursor solution is deposited onto the substrate by spin coating and/or spray coating. 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein the substrate comprises a semiconductor substrate comprising a semiconductor selected from the group consisting of T1O2, CmO, CuFeCk, Si, InGaN, InGaP, InP, and combinations thereof. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the substrate comprises CU2O. 19. The method of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the substrate comprises a conductive substrate comprising Cu, Ag, Au, Fe, Ti, Al, Pt, C, indium doped tin oxide (ITO), fluorine doped tin oxide (FTO), or a combination thereof. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the conductive substrate comprises FTO. 21. The method of any one of claims 1-20, wherein the substrate comprises a layer comprising a semiconductor disposed on a conductive substrate and wherein the 

 semiconductor is selected from the group consisting of T1O2, Cu 2 0, CuFeC , Si, InGaN, InGaP, InP, and combinations thereof. 22. The method of claim 21, further comprising forming the substrate by electrodepositing the semiconductor layer onto the conductive substrate. 23. The method of any one of claims 17-22, wherein the semiconductor comprises a plurality of particles having an average particle size of from 5 nm to 10 pm, or from 1 pm to 2 pm. 24. The method of any one of claims 17-23, wherein the semiconductor has an average thickness of 5 nm to 10 pm, or from 1 pm to 3 pm. 25. The method of any one of claims 1-24, wherein thermally annealing the precursor electrode comprises contacting the precursor electrode with an annealing gas at an annealing temperature. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the annealing temperature is from 50Â°C to 600Â°C, from 350Â°C to 500Â°C, or from 400Â°C to 500Â°C. 27. The method of claim 25 or claim 26, wherein the annealing gas comprises an inert gas. 28. The method of any one of claims 25-27, wherein the annealing gas comprises N 2 . 29. The method of any one of claims 1-28, wherein the precursor electrode is thermally annealed for an amount of time from 10 seconds to 12 hours. 30. The method of any one of claims 1-29, wherein the M0S2 layer has an average thickness of from 1 nm to 200 nm, or from 30 nm to 40 nm. 31. A composite electrode made by the method of any one of claims 1-30. 32. The composite electrode of claim 31, wherein the composite electrode has a photocurrent density of at least 2.0 mA cm 2 at 0 V vs. a reversible hydrogen electrode. 33. The composite electrode of claim 31 or claim 32, wherein the composite electrode has a dark current onset potential of -0.9 V or less as measured against a saturated calomel electrode. 34. The composite electrode of any one of claims 31-33, wherein the composite electrode has a power efficiency of from 1% to 5%. 35. The composite electrode of any one of claims 31-34, wherein the composite electrode has a stability of 91% after 10 minutes in near-neutral electrolyte. 36. A method of use of the composite electrode of any of the claims 31-35 as an electrode in an electrolysis or photoelectrochemical reaction. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the composite electrode is used as an electrode in a water splitting reaction. 38. A method of use of the composite electrode of any one of claims 31-35 as an electrode in a solar cell. 39. A method of use of the composite electrode of any one of claims 31-35 as an electrode in a charge storage device. 40. A photoelectrochemical cell comprising: a working electrode comprising the composite electrode of any one of claims 31-35 in electrochemical contact with a liquid sample; and one or more additional electrodes in electrochemical contact with the liquid sample. 41. The photoelectrochemical cell of claim 40, wherein the liquid sample comprises water. 42. A method of use of the photoelectrochemical cell of claim 40 for a water splitting reaction. 43. The method of claim 42, wherein the water splitting reaction comprises solar water splitting. 44. A device comprising the composite electrode of any one of claims 31-35, wherein the device comprises a sensor, an energy conversion device, or a combination thereof. 45. An energy conversion device comprising the composite electrode of any one of claims 31-35, wherein the energy conversion device comprises a solar cell, a fuel cell, a photovoltaic cell, or a combination thereof.
